# Implement dual-search read-path fallback in LocalFileService

## Context
This is bucket B2 of Epic #11187.
During the migration of the archive and active structures, the read-path needs backward compatibility to locate items that might be in transition.

## The Problem
If `LocalFileService` only looks in the new deterministic paths, it will fail to find items that haven't been moved yet.

## The Architectural Reality
- `ai/services/github-workflow/LocalFileService.mjs`

## The Fix
Implement a dual-search in `LocalFileService` to fall back to legacy paths if an item is not found in the new canonical path.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `LocalFileService#getIssueById` (and PR equivalent) checks both old and new paths.

## Out of Scope
- Modifying the write-path or syncer logic.

## Related
- Epic #11187

Origin Session ID: 2c4aa4df-2628-45ae-a9c2-156fd9308f21

- 2026-05-13T10:18:13Z @tobiu referenced in commit `48456be` - "feat(github-workflow): dual-search read-path in LocalFileService for archive transition (#11285) (#11289)

Implements B2 of Epic #11187 archive substrate migration. During the active-to-archive
transition, LocalFileService read-path now tries the new canonical archiveRoot before
falling back to legacy archiveDir for issues, and recurses into legacy XXxx subdirs of
discussionsDir before searching archiveRoot for discussions.

- getIssueById: tries archiveRoot first, falls back to archiveDir
- getDiscussionById: tries flat discussionsDir first, falls back to recursive XXxx subdirs,
  then archiveRoot (no legacy discussion-archive substrate exists per Epic #11187 body)
- 10 unit tests covering all dual-search paths + ordering (new canonical wins on duplicate)

Test discipline: describe.serial to prevent fullyParallel races on shared aiConfig
mutations per feedback_symmetric_spec_cleanup memory anchor.

Verified: 80/80 pass across test/playwright/unit/ai/services/github-workflow/ — no
regression in sibling test suites.
